cards.back: A hash function is a one-way mathematical function that takes input data of any size and produces a fixed-size output, known as a message digest or digest value

cards.front: What is the purpose of a hash function?
cards.back: To verify the integrity and authenticity of data by producing a unique, fixed-size output that is difficult to alter or manipulate

A problem in probability theory that illustrates the surprising ease with which two or more people can share the same birthdate, highlighting the limitations of assumptions of independence and randomness

cards.front: What is the probability of two people sharing the same birthday?
cards.back: Approximately 0.2915 (or 29.15%) in a group of 23 people, demonstrating how quickly the probability of shared birthdays increases as the group size grows

cards.front: Why is the Birthday Problem relevant to hash functions?
cards.back: Because hash functions rely on the idea that collisions (shared outputs) are rare, but the Birthday Problem illustrates that collisions are more likely than expected, making hash functions less secure
